channel.name: hardware-tpms
channel.topic: Channel is to discuss about moving into a hardware TPM role and to discuss the intersection between hardware and software.

messages.content: Soo anyone work for NVIDIA ? 👀👀👀
Are you finding that Hardware TPM has less TC than other TPMs?
Is this true?
I think HW TPMs make less than SW TPMs and more in line with top band of HW engineers. Apple is a great company to study on Levelsfyi. I have seen this trend at Tesla too.
I’m a HW TPM btw
Getting tired of this disparity when both ( HW and SW) are equally critical...
It’s market driven. Launching Software fundamentally carries low overhead vs HW.
Im an analog ic design engineer right now. Any pointers on moving into hardware tpm role?
Do i need to do an mba? Read books? Work ex ?
Im completely clueless
Please help
Yoe = 5 yrs, 4 at intel , 1 at small company
HW TPM at Apple here. My comp is decent FYI.
Imo - transfer internally within your company. That’s what I did
What is your YOE and comp ? @Borderline88
Mine is 280K for 6YOE + Masters
I have $200k for 4yoe. Internally is hard given its a design house
I need to move first to a big company and then move internally i guess
Can i apply for a tpm role in other companies?
Where do you work @Solenya
Sorry can’t reveal. Company is too small and it’s become obvious
@Borderline88 apple EPM = TPM, correct?
Where are you at? Comoany and area
Where are you at?
Its a small design house in the bay. Private company not a startup
We do analog designs only
Okay

12YOE (9y Eng, 2y EM, 6mo EPM) 
210 base, 500 RSU, bonus TBD but heard 15-20% is meet expectation
Yes

@Yals this is how I transitioned from purely technical roles to TPM. I've seen a few others done it the same way, so I'd like to give you something to start with.

1) Work alongside TPMs and make sure you really like the R&R.
2) Find internal opportunities for managing a program. Official title change is nice (easier in smaller companies) but it's the experience that matters. 
3) Keep notes about how your day to day R&R help move a program forward as it'd be critical for your resume/interview later.
4) Start applying for TPM roles once you deliver one or two programs and feel ready to convince others why you'd make a good TPM.
5) Focus on the job that has the best match to your technical background as you can't compete on PM experience.
6) Expect a ton of rejections and no-replies since recruiters are swamped with resume from people with TPM titles.
7) Rehearse your interviews and do mocks as much as possible
8) Repeat 4) to 7) until you get the offer you want.
Thanks a lot @Borderline88 . Just a noob question- what’s R&R?
Roles & Responsibilities
Why did you change from EM to EPM ? Wanted to go back to being an IC ?
Thanks @Solenya

It was a hard decision for me to make coz I really loved my team. At the end of the day, it was between a EM position under a project that has no resources or HW TPM at Apple.
I chose the latter for future prospects and a piece of mind.
How many HW TPMs do we have here? Can you y’all add a thumbs up here ?
I have a background in both hardware and SW/cloud dev
What are some non-tech roles to go after?
Material planning
Very fewer companies have hardware EPM/TPM roles that requires less years of work exp, so if someone have the list ?
Materials PM, Operations PM, Global Supply Manager, Supply Chain PM
We’re hiring manufacturing pm at Lyft (transit bikes and scooters business unit): https://www.linkedin.com/jobs/view/2801694386
Apple has many Ops PM role. One example here: https://jobs.apple.com/en-us/search?location=united-states-USA

messages.content: I am curious to know about the interview process and preparation for hardware TPMs? Is the technical interview still system design based?
I recently interviewed for nuro and tesla and personally didn’t see system design covered for either role. However, systems thinking and system science background always a helpful.
No system design, but you need to have some domain knowledge, from design to qualification
Got it, thank you!
Yeah I’ve interviewed a lot and system design is outside of hardware domain. But they will ask you decision making questions

Even I interviewed with Tesla earlier & no system design questions. However, I want to learn more about System thinking/System science related to Hardware/Consumer Electronics Products, so any good resources for that?
^+1. Count me in
Would also like to see if we can form infra/HW study and interview groups
I think it’ll be super productive to work with us fellow TPMs
And sorry I’m a little late to this but HAPPY NEW YEAR everyone! May your TCs be bigger than your targets this year and for years to come.
Any Fb/Meta hardware TPMs here who can help to educate interview process? I have an recruiter screening call tomorrow. What to expect?
Any updates? I’m also a hw tpm, wondering how the screening went.
Hey.. the initial recruiter screening went well. General resume , background questions. Why Meta now? Next week I have 45mins phone screen with another TPM.
@Dan2 - I have a phone interview scheduled for next week also, as Infrastructure TPM.  If you need any help with mock interview etc. please let me know.  It will be helpful for my preparation also.
Sounds good. Please check DM we can finalize some time.

Hey I had my 45 min TPM last week. It looked a lot like the interviewer had a long list of questions they had to run through. Here’s my interview: 
1. Introduce yourself
2. Program you initiated and managed 
3. System design of the project [ explained my system + release process ] 
4. A project you disagreed with a teammate and you were wrong. 
5. A project that involved multiple cross functional teams? 
6. A project that involved a technical challenge [ it was an extension question asking me to define an API customization in depth ]. 
He gave me 5 minutes to ask my questions and noted them down too. 
Hope this helps.
Was this for a sw tpm or a hw tpm? For #3 was that related to the project from #2 or did they give you a topic?

Finished my Meta 45min TPM phone screen today for Hardware Compute TPM - Interview continued for an 1 hour 
1. Introduce yourself
2. Explain system architecture (blocks, interconnects, peripherals) for a program you managed 
3. Explain an program managed end-to-end. He asked lot of follow up questions on defining scope, managing risk, drafting the PRD
4. Scenario where you saw major risk item in execution close to a critical phase exit. how did you navigate the program?
5. How do you manage conflicts and disagreements with critical stakeholders

messages.content:
Hello folks sharing my experience for the Meta TPM interview. Giving back to the wonderful community here who has helped me at every stage of this process. Thanks🙏 😀  
Received YES from the recruiter last Friday for IC5 HW TPM. In negotiation phase now.
#meta #interview-experience - HW Infra TPM - Compute AI/ML hardware
Recruiter call - 
-Basic resume questions, Overall experience,Explain Project managed E2E, Why Meta? Why now?
-Other Interview in pipeline? Any other offers? Salary expectations

Phone Screen  - 
https://discord.com/channels/853099205206999050/921572854745694239/933448919034253312

Interview panel Loop - 
1. System Design 
-Infra hardware requirement to meet new feature requirements for a new FB app feature? How will you start tackling the scope with the internal customer?
-Asked assumptions on benchmarks/workload, Storage, Network, Compute, PCIe requirements
-Thermal/Power/Cooling analysis
-Scalability/Serviceability features in hardware?
-Rack design, power consumption, Scale in vs Scale out
-Really great interviewer he was positive from the beginning and provided good direction

2. Leadership
-Why Meta reasons?
-Opinion different from team: how did you convince ?
-Took on additional responsibility and proactive solutions implemented ?
-Project where you failed? Why do you consider it YOUR failure?
-How you manage declining performance from a stakeholder

3. Program Sense
-How you kickoff program with little or no references
-How do you get buy-in from program
-Program where Goal was missed? Why? - few follow up questions

4. Tech Retro
-Basic x86 architecture block diagram - different subsystems DDR, PCIe, PCH, BMC
-Complex program hw design and key components - went into PSU design
-PCIe card failing in field? How do you resolve

5. Partnerships
-How do you get a successful consensus among key partners?
-Hard decision taken as a team?
-Team stakeholder gating progress- how do you manage
-Example of a mistake made in a program
Pinned a message.

Ya.  Referral always better. DM me for meta

Sure
hey yall what does a hardware tpm do? I am new grad and cs background and would like to know more about how hardware tpm is different from sw tpm.  I couldnt find lot of information online.
Count me in. Please share the schedule.
Anyone?

The overall principles are similar but the situational focus can be different. In general, hw tpms spend more time on understanding the supply chain/procurement/manufacturing as these are important factors to program success. HW tpms may also rely on waterfall method to assess program progress in my observation, while agile methods have been employed to assist (it can be reversed in some environments). HW tpms may also have a higher chance to work with or manage suppliers/partners as part of job duties. Here's also my observation: A good hw tpm can gain engineering team's trust, and sometimes even dependence more easily, as the complexity the engineers face naturally will force them to ask for some leverage from else where. Again, my observation in my experience merely 🙂
thankss, i really appreciate it

I am would like to bring some clarity to the Hardware TPM role. Here are some things I need help with -

Would you recommend any HW TPM/PM managers I should speak with ?

To see more mock TPM interviews, you can browse our YouTube Playlist: http...

messages.content: for your first question - topics can be capacity planning for new green field projects specifically hardware centric.. metrics used to estimate/measure data center utilization..
Thanks

You might want to get some one in the Hardware NPI space who deploy platform to Data center. For example, starting from defining the Hardware Architecture (Requirements, scope etc) to  development, deployment and sustaining of a  hardware platform and challenges faced during EVT/DVT/PVT/MP. Getting people from OCP (Meta, Google) would be a good start.
Hey. I have a phone screen with an AV company for a TPM role. Any advice on what to expect would be greatly appreciated! Thanks
You may have to specify which company so people can understand the context better...
Sorry the company is Cruise. I wasn’t sure what the policy on naming companies here is

I recently had a video screen with another AV company's hiring manager. In the interview, he asked me the break down of the module, failure mode of the product, hardware & physics limitation, design for reliability under timeframe & how to reduce time for implementation
hope that helps
Thanks!!
I have an upcoming interview for hardware TPM role in AWS. I was wondering if system design is asked for hardware role.
is it for HM or loop?

I got ahead of myself in the wording there. Right now, I have an introductory call scheduled with the recruiter this week. But wanted to ask ahead of time, incase I am able to clear the initial rounds.
I have an upcoming phone screen interview for a hardware TPM role at Google. Any advice for preparation?
What kind of HW TPM role?
It’s most probably a hardware TPM role in the Google Pixel team. Recruiter said he is keeping the options open so that the chances of team match are high.

Question for HW TPMs - do you engage in system design at all ? IMO it has always been a system architect/engineer that did this and then I would be the “customer” that made sense of it - primarily focusing on what are we trying to do and how doers this system help us do it.
oh Google is still hiring? I thought they were on a freeze?
Appears so
Not really. It's mostly about the know-how in the specific technical areas. For example, managing design change and tool modification for PD TPM and board-spin and bring-up for EE TPM.

while each company and how they define TPM/EPM is a bit different. i personally feel i have a better control/understanding of the bottleneck or risk if I have some of the technical know-hows (eg. mechanical design, manufacturing processes, EE layout /E3 environment, integration) . I also think that helps me understand the system's capabilities, which comes in handy during verification/validation

anyone interviewed/ing for SD for hardware/embedded TPM roles ? What kind of system design questions could expect?
YOE-12 prepping for L5/L6 MAANG
most SD examples are distributed or cloud computing related. not much hardware centric for TPM roles

Thermal considerations, power considerations, time/Schedule, type of chips/radios to use..  for e.g. there was a question here on design a bird species detection based on sound in forest. I have seen design an internet system for remote island in Hawaii, design supermarket checkout system, design an elevator, design a tablet for blind people, design traffic lights ,

Consider things like switches that have a binary band for on and off which experience physical fluttering between the two when it’s partially pressed and how you’d work around that was one embedded question I got. Another was to explain how I’d update a specific ecu on a chain in canbus with a specific rate on the network and a bunch of specifics about the processors involved
Also got more generalized systems design questions around designing a platform for IOT devices on a global CatM1 network.
Anyones Teams have open reqs for HW TPMs ?
I saw a lot of openings in Apple. They are not hiring for everything but probably selective roles. Especially silicon side
FYI most of these roles are strictly onsite in their Orange County office. At least they were a few months ago.
Tesla is still hiring for quite a bit of TPM/EPM positions.
they are doing really well financially and should be fine during recession
+5% after hours today after Q4 earnings call
Any NVIDIA HW TPMs here ? Would love to connect and learn more about the workflow and culture. I’ve spent almost a decade in EV/AV (Tesla + Nuro) and wanna branch out.

Hi hw TPMs. Have a meta FRL interview coming up for AIML team. I was told that I’ll be interviewed for a solution design interview as part of the process. This is my first time prepping for a solution design without any background for AIML. Can you folks help me with resources that can help me crack this interview? Tia

From what I remember, your recruiter can help a lot with providing details of what expectations might be, so definitely ask there.

Can you be more specific about your concerns? Is it because you don’t have an AIML background, or you aren’t sure about solution design for interviews , or something else?
@Forrest Fairbrian I'm purely a HW TPM with no AIML exposure and with experience in manufacturing. As well never been asked on system/solution designs in the past interview experiences
Do you have a link to the opening?

I think your best bet is to just in general look at solution design interview prep material to get a sense for the ground rules of breaking down and working out a solution.

That’ll get you 75% of the way there. After that there may be some practice that is relevant to the specific opening/relevant problems for the position. If you have the opening that may help.

messages.content: Hi, I am a Senior TPM with experience across both HW & SW and trying to crack a Sata Canter HW TPM role with focus on technical program management along with supply chain operations. I have had multiple interviews including Meta but I am not able to go pass the hiring manager & technical screen rounds. I am not sure if my answers are conveying enough seniority/ I am being impactful in my stories. Any help is appreciated

I am assuming you meant Data center TPM.  I don’t have much experience with Meta but for behavioral, STARR or CARL method is good resource and your stories/ answers need to focus on business impact either in the form of  $ saved or $ created. This is a general view based on my experience as HW TPM and in Silicon operations as well. DM if you want to talk.
provide your successful tpm story  which will align with data center TPM role and we can comment here.

Good read 
https://ai.meta.com/blog/meta-mtia-scale-ai-chips-for-billions/
